article_title: Responsibilities along the AI value chain
obligation_text: Under the conditions laid down in this Article, a distributor, importer, deployer, or other third party shall be considered to be a provider of a high-risk AI system and shall be subject to the obligations of the provider if they: put their name or trademark on a high-risk AI system already placed on the market or put into service; make a substantial modification to a high-risk AI system; or modify the intended purpose of an AI system which is not classified as high-risk, resulting in it becoming high-risk.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI value chain liability triggers, including conditions under which rebranding, substantial modification, or intended purpose modification transfers provider obligations to other value chain actors.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI value chain liability triggers, including conditions under which rebranding, substantial modification, or intended purpose modification transfers provider obligations to other value chain actors.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI governance structures, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 25(1) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.511, cosine: 0.587). Secondary: LS-K-007 (LS, cosine: 0.572), LS-K-012 (LS, cosine: 0.568).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.1/10 (raw 0.511). Shared vocabulary: [ai, conditions, knowledge]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [actors, chain, intended, liability, modification, obligations] vs WIDAI terms [accountability, assignment, deployment, enable, governance, lifecycle] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.863 (P=0.852, R=0.875). R exceeds P by 0.023 — the WIDAI KSA's 25 words are well-captured by the competency implication's 26 words, but the competency implication contains broader scope.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.5%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.587 > STS 0.511 (gap +0.076). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, conditions, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.2%. Shared tokens: [ai, conditions, knowledge]. EU AI Act-only: [actors, chain, intended, liability, modification, obligations]. WIDAI-only: [accountability, assignment, deployment, enable, governance, lifecycle].
